response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with strong testing culture and full-stack breadth, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; his resume shows only a brief 7-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project. His systems experience (high-throughput DLang microservices at Dunnhumby) is relevant but adjacent, and his recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node product engineering rather than large-scale compute infrastructure. He also lacks demonstrated formal technical-leadership experience mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers on a platform team.
strengths: Experience with low-latency, high-throughput microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second (DLang, async I/O)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including open-source platform ownership on Kubernetes
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++ experience (roughly under 1 year of professional C/C++)
No dedicated cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership; recent roles are product-focused full-stack work
No formal technical-lead role leading cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance engineering exposure
